title: Designing a Chatbot for Mental Health Support with Emotion Detection
scenario: Design a chatbot for mental health support that can detect and respond to user emotions using natural language processing (NLP) techniques. The chatbot must be able to recognize and classify user emotions, provide empathetic responses, and recommend resources for mental health support. The output should be a Python script (as code) implementing the emotion detection and response algorithm. Constraints: Must use a NLP library (e.g., NLTK), must use a machine learning library (e.g., scikit-learn), and must be under 300 lines of code. The evaluation criteria are: (1) accuracy of emotion detection (40 points), (2) appropriateness of empathetic responses (25 points), (3) effectiveness of resource recommendations (20 points), (4) maintainability of code (5 points), and (5) adherence to best practices (10 points).
constraints: Must use a NLP library (e.g., NLTK)
Must use a machine learning library (e.g., scikit-learn)
Must be under 300 lines of code

evaluationCriteria.description: Evaluates the accuracy of emotion detection using NLP techniques.
Assesses the appropriateness and empathy of the chatbot's responses.
Checks the effectiveness of resource recommendations provided by the chatbot.
Evaluates the maintainability of the code in terms of readability, modularity, and testability.
Checks if the code adheres to best practices in terms of security, performance, and code organization.
